provisions.title: Tittle and application
provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the Moshi District Council (De­velopment levy) By-laws 2002 and shall be deemed io have come into operation on the first day of January, 2002.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these By-laws, unless otherwise the context requires:— " Act " means the Local Government Finances Act, 1982, No. 9 of 1982 " businessperson " means any person, 'legal' or 'natural' who is carrying on business under any written Law in the area of jurisdiction of the Coun­cil; " development levy " means tire annual rate designated as Development levy and Payable under the provisions of these By-laws and in accordance with the Act ; " council " means the Moshi District Council; " agent " means a duly authorised person or persons for the collection of the Development levy for or on behalf of the Council as established under the Act . " employee " means any person in the employment of another person " employer " means any person who is the Director, Owner, Head of, Supervisor and, or who has the final decision on the matters of any institution or office that carries on its business in the area of the jurisdiction of the Council and whose employees have the duty to pay the Develop­ment Levy.

provisions.title: Jurisdiction
provisions.content: With effect from the first day of January 2002, every person who is a resident within the area of Jurisdiction of Moshi District Council and who is liable to pay levy made by the Council, shall pay to the Council a Levy of Tanzanian Shillings Three thousands only (3,000/=) per annum.

provisions.title: Levy
provisions.content: Every Businessperson shall be liable to pay a Development Levy of T. Shillings Three thousands (3,000/-) per annum to the Council.

provisions.title: Last day of paying levy
provisions.content: The 30 th day of September of each calendar year shall be the last day of which every person liable to pay the levy shall have paid it to the Council.

provisions.title: Penalty
provisions.content: Where after the 30 th day of September of each calendar year, any person liable to pay the Levy shall have not paid it, he shall be liable, in addition to the levy, to pay a penalty of fifty per centum (50%) of the levy payable by him.

provisions.title: Agents
provisions.content: The Council may use the services of Agents in collection of the levy and it shall thus be the duty of any levy payer to pay the levy to the Agent who has been authorised by the Council.

provisions.title: Register of levy payers
provisions.content: The Council shall prepare and have a register of all Levy payers liable to pay the levy and it shall be the duty of every male person of eighteen (18) years and above to register in the Levy Payers Register at his respective Village or Ward.

provisions.title: Offences
provisions.content: Any person who, without any lawful justification or excuse, incites any person to refuse to pay the Levy payable by him under these By-laws after it becomes due and payable, or who incites or assists any person to misrepresent his rateable capacity commits an offence under these By­laws.
Any person required to give information, shall give all such Information as may be reasonably required by the Council, Agent, Rate collector or Assessment Committee within the required time.
Any person who, having been required to give information under these By-laws neglects, refuses or, without lawful excuse, fails to give the information required, commits an offence.

Any Agent who, appointed to collect the Levy, fails to deposit with the Council the Levy collected, or knowingly demands from any person an amount in excess of the authorised levy or recklessly or knowingly renders false returns, whether orally or in writing, of the number of Levy payers or the amount of Levy collected, commits and offence.

Any person who contravenes or fails to comply with any of the Provisions of these By-laws, shall be guilty of an Offence and upon con­viction shall be liable to a fine ranging from Tanzanian Shillings fifteen thousands (15,000/-) to and not exceeding Tanzanian Shillings Fifty thou­sands (50,000/-) only or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or to both such fine and imprisonment.

provisions.title: Revocation of G.N No.35 of 1997 and 176 of 2000
provisions.content: The Moshi District Council (Graduated Development Levy) By-laws, 1997 and the Moshi District Council (Graduated Development levy) (amendments) By laws, 2000, are hereby revoked.
